title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Please watch this entire video before caring for your drain.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: This video will teach you how to take care of your surgical drain.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Curgical drains remove fluid from under your skin near your surgical site.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: This helps prevent infection and encourages healing.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: A surgical drain consists of a drain tube that runs from your surgical site

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: to a bulb that collects the fluid.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: You strip the drain tube to keep it clear and remove any clots or blockages.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Then you empty the bulb when it is half full or as instructed by your healthcare provider.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: You will need alcohol wipes, a measuring cup and a drainage record sheet.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Be sure to wear disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Be careful not to pull on the tubing.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: You should not feel any tugging where the tube enters your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 1. Wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 2. Put on disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 3. Look for clots or blockages that may prevent the fluid from flowing out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Some clots may be hidden inside the tube under your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 4. Loosen the clots by gently squeezing the tubes surrounding them.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 5. Use one hand to hold the drain tube in place where it leaves your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 6. Use your other hand to pinch the tube with an alcohol wipe between your finger and thumb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 7. Slide your pinched fingers along the tube to force any fluid out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: You may need to repeat steps 5 through 7 several times to clear the tube.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Try not to let go of the tube between steps.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: If fluid remains in the tube or you accidentally let go, repeat steps 5 through 7 using a new alcohol wipe.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Do not allow the bulb to become more than half full.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Too much fluid in the bulb reduces its ability to remove fluid from underneath your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Now you will begin emptying the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 8. Hold the bulb lower than your incision so that fluid moves out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 9. Point the bulb away from your body.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Never squeeze the bulb before taking the cap off.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 10. Remove the cap.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Never touch the opening with your bare hands.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 11. Hold the measuring cup under the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 12. Turn the bulb upside down and squeeze the fluid into the cup.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 13. After removing the fluid, continue squeezing the bulb and use a new alcohol wipe to clean the top.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 14. While still squeezing the bulb, put the cap back on the top.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: The depressed bulb creates suction that continuously removes fluid from underneath your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 15. Read the amount of fluid in the measuring cup.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 16. Write the amount on your record sheet.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Step 17. Empty and rinse the cup as directed.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Keep the bulb below the level of your incision to help the fluid move out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice the amount of fluid suddenly increases or decreases, the odor of the fluid changes.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: The fluid contains pus or becomes thicker over time.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Your drain tube falls out or your incision opens.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
text_en: Your incision is red, swollen, painful or has pus coming out or your temperature is 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.
